# Golden Eagle Log Homes — Engagement Overview

edits.new_value:
# Golden Eagle Log Homes — Engagement Overview

Golden Eagle Log Homes designs and sells log homes. Their customers are typically in the top 10% of earners. The product is very expensive, with sales cycles of 1–3+ years (some prospects watch for up to 8 years before engaging). The company culture is "multiple hats" — everyone wears several roles.

## Key Discovery Call — 2026-03-27

Attendees: Jay Eichinger (owner), Tammy, Lucas Eichinger (sales staff), Andy Eichinger (pricing), Sean Flaherty (IT manager), Chris Stitcher (general manager), Sarah Tarnutter. Consulting side: Anna, Michael Hunter Daniel. Juan (Anna's father) made the introduction.

## Priority Areas of Interest

### 1. Photorealistic AI Renderings / Walkthroughs
Jay's "biggest dream" — take floor plans and produce photorealistic AI-rendered video walkthroughs indistinguishable from real home tours, for use on the website. They currently have a couple of people doing renderings, but Jay says they're "not realistic enough" and it "takes too long."

### 2. AI in Marketing
Jay wants help figuring out how to use AI in marketing. Currently spending ~$600,000/year total on marketing (including labor, video production by Jay's son Luke, search advertising, etc.).

- **YouTube is the #1 channel.** 99% of inbound customers say they found Golden Eagle via YouTube home tour videos. Jay's son Luke (not to be confused with stepson Lucas) is a videographer who produces all video content. Spending ~$15,000–$18,000/month promoting YouTube videos. Recently YouTube clicks have been down.
- **Paid search via JumpFly.** ~5,500 paid keywords. Almost no customers attribute their call to Google ads. Jay feels paid search is largely ineffective currently.
- **SEO via JumpFly.** Paying JumpFly's SEO division for organic search optimization — they have 2–3 women working on it for ~1.5 years. About 2 years ago they completely rebuilt the website; it looks better but they lost all prior SEO performance. Organic search keeps declining despite investment. Jay suspects AI search is bypassing them.
- **Facebook.** Sean and Jay boost Facebook posts (not formal ads). Followers grew by 100,000 in the last year. Leads from Facebook are lower quality. Housing ad restrictions (age/income discrimination rules) make targeting difficult.
- **Instagram.** Luke's team handles posting. Audience may not yet be buyers but Jay is "not down on it."
- **TikTok.** Also present on TikTok.
- **LinkedIn, X, Pinterest, Houzz.** Lucas's wife does part-time work expanding presence on these platforms for ~6–7 months, to improve discoverability by AI search.
- **Legacy channels (trade shows, log home magazines):** Virtually no response anymore; largely discontinued.

### 3. SEO/Analytics Automation (Sean's idea)
Sean wants to build an AI bot/agent that could: study keyword lists, analyze competitors' websites, compare to their own site, generate daily reports — potentially replacing the need for an SEO firm. He's been "dipping his toe into the OpenAI world" but hasn't gotten far yet. Also interested in AI monitoring social media comments across YouTube, Facebook, Instagram and generating consensus sentiment reports, plus helping draft responses.

### 4. Analytics
Currently weak on analytics — rely on JumpFly's Google Analytics reports. Jay and Sean are skeptical of analytics value but open to an easier approach. JumpFly does some phone number tracking for certain ads. Long sales cycles make attribution very difficult.

### 5. Sales Lead Response Time
Juan flagged that lead response time may lag by hours, possibly up to 24 hours. He noted it should be near-immediate.

### 6. Meeting Recording with Customers
Juan mentioned meeting recording as a topic to discuss (not yet elaborated in this call).

### 7. General AI Enablement
Juan shared that at his own company he made Claude usage mandatory for all employees 3 months ago, purchased licenses for everyone, and requires it for every job function. He encouraged Golden Eagle to consider similar company-wide AI adoption.

## No Social Listening Currently
No keyword listening on social sites (Twitter, Reddit, etc.) — Sean noted he doesn't even know what that means. Opportunity area identified.

Jay personally responds to YouTube comments. Passionate about getting photorealistic AI renderings of floor plans. Skeptical of analytics but open-minded. Acknowledges they're spending ~$600K/year on marketing and knows a lot is wasted, but wants to clean it up. Suspects AI search is disrupting their organic traffic.

Was introduced to the consulting team by Juan (Anna's father). Jay had been telling Juan about interest in AI for marketing and expressed concern about declining search performance.

# Golden Eagle Log Homes — Engagement Overview

## Current Phase: Discovery & Training (Sprint 2)

Kicking off with a discovery and training phase. Conducting six interviews with the sales team, plus ad hoc interviews with key stakeholders (Jay, Sean, Tammy, and others). Estimated ~10-12 hours of interview work.

Debrief presentation scheduled for Friday. Prep work planned for Thursday (roughly a half day). Friday debrief expected to be about an hour.

Goal: deeply understand what needs to be built, deliver AI training, and produce a proposal informed by discovery findings.

## Client Context

- The client is a family business — roughly six family members involved. Decision-making will likely be a committee decision, with Jay having final say.
- They are using Microsoft Teams for meetings.
- Tammy is the primary point of contact / "confident" on the client side. She confirmed interest in starting with the sales group.
- Client's father is building the CRM separately; the client expects to integrate with that work. Tammy already lumps both efforts into one big project.
- The client wants the tech (AI tooling for sales), but the engagement is broader: organizational and AI initiative covering tech, training, enablement, adoption, and techniques.

## What We're Building (Planned)

Three-phase AI sales tool around call intelligence:

1. **During the call** — Diarized transcription (using OpenAI Whisper + Pyannote for diarization), running on own servers. Need to check if Teams' native transcription capabilities are sufficient before building custom.
2. **Post-call** — Automated generation of assets: key decisions, action items, follow-up emails, summaries.
3. **Pre-next-call** — Pre-meeting briefs: recent signals, customer overview, conversation history, rapport hooks. Designed as a doc to glance at five minutes before a call.
4. **CRM integration** — Connecting all outputs to the CRM.

### Technical Decisions & Considerations

- Evaluate Teams' native transcription/recording before building custom pipeline (Teams provides raw video file + transcript file that can feed a data pipeline).
- For round one, transcription can run on own server racks.
- Not building yet — need to validate with users first. Plan is to build clickable prototypes / mockups showing what the output would look like.
- Output format matters more than speed: PDF? Email? Slack message? Teams message? Need to discover this in interviews.
- Follow-up delivery speed: doesn't need to be instant (5 seconds vs 10 minutes doesn't matter much), but 4+ hours is too long.
- Need to ask in conversation guides: what formats do reps want? What data sources are most useful? Is the prep doc useful?

## Pricing

- Current engagement: discovery and training phase (price discussed with client, no invoice sent yet but pricing was communicated).
- If this phase goes well, a follow-on proposal around ~$15,000 should sell itself.
- Aspirational: $50,000 contract for expanded scope including marketing tools, visualizations of houses, self-updating website, etc.

## This Week's Plan

1. Schedule and conduct all sales rep interviews (target: complete by mid-day Thursday).
2. Build conversation guides — one for sales reps, one for key stakeholders (Jay, Sean, Tammy).
3. Have individual meeting with Tammy.
4. Prep for Friday debrief/presentation (Thursday).
5. Deliver Friday debrief presentation.
6. Consider building a demo for Friday showing the system in action (Teams call → real-time transcription → action items → follow-up generation). Budget: ~1-1.5 days max.
7. Begin developing training to deliver the following week.

## Proposal Structure

Organized into three areas:
- **Sales** — primary focus for now (wish list, needs, proposed deliverables)
- **Marketing** — not being touched in current phase; potential phase two. Exploring partnership with a YC company doing AI marketing optimization for services businesses (could resell if software-heavy, or competitive if services-heavy).
- **Team Enablement**

## Invoicing

Discussing whether to invoice via father's company (The Automatic Office — possibly Delaware or Florida entity) or via Accelerate Holdings LLC (Texas LLC). Need to sort out payment flow to avoid unnecessary tax accounting complexity.
edits.source_quote: We kick off with a discovery and training phase... We're doing six interviews with the sales team... we'll probably have a couple of other just like ad hoc interviews... we also are going to do a debrief with them on Friday... they're using teams... Jay is going to have basically final say... The holding corpus called the automatic office... Accelerate holding LLC... it's a Texas LLC

edits.new_value:
# Golden Eagle Log Homes — Engagement Overview

## Current Focus
Starting with the **sales piece** of meetings — recording meetings and ensuring everyone has the necessary context, action items, and transcripts.

## Product / Dashboard Concept
A meeting intelligence dashboard that includes:
- **Call history**: List of all past calls with dates, transcripts, summaries, and key moments
- **What changed per call**: Updates generated from each meeting (e.g., tasks like "send post-call email" with ready-to-send drafts)
- **Project files (left panel)**: Per-person notes (e.g., about each client — their profession, preferences, etc.) and a project overview that updates across time as calls happen. Version history is available, and everything is editable.
- **Upcoming meetings (right panel)**: Meeting objective, recent signals, open items, and prep material
- **AI assistant**: Integrated chat that has full context

## Architecture & Hosting
- Can initially live on our servers (data center) or be mounted on the client's own servers
- Main requirements: audio storage (audios can be deleted over time), APIs for generated assets
- Possible integration with **Microsoft Graph API** for email, calendar, or other Microsoft ecosystem features

## Audio / Transcript Capture
Three options discussed for obtaining audio and transcripts:
1. **Meeting bot** — e.g., a bot that joins calls (like Fireflies), but would be reinventing the wheel
2. **Microsoft Graph API** — client records all meetings in Teams; use the API to retrieve recordings (provides free transcription and diarization)
3. **System audio capture** — capture audio entering and leaving the computer regardless of platform

For **Teams and Zoom** calls, the native transcript from those tools should suffice. For **phone calls and in-person meetings**, the solution is still TBD.

**Recall.ai** was discussed as a comprehensive API for transcripts and recordings across platforms. Decision: **not including Recall in the stack** — agreed not to add a third-party dependency here.

## Dad's System Design Work
Dad is working on the broader CRM and the design/engineering/accounting process. He is currently in **system design phase** — understanding the major entities, how they interact, and the process flows. He is not yet at the implementation/storage stage.

The **central entity** of the whole system is the house design, captured in a form called the **211D**, which contains every part of the house. Everything flows from this:
- From the first sales call, a workspace opens around this entity
- Client works with design and sales teams to shape the house (floors, rooms, materials, style, wall heights, etc.)
- From the 211D, **engineering, pricing, payment plans, proposals, selections, contracts, and delivery** all derive
- Each field has **change history tracking** with timestamps and reasons (e.g., "customer change"), solving the problem where changes discussed in sales calls weren't propagated to the 10+ documents referencing old values

Phases in the system: Project overview → Design (211D) → Ballpark pricing → Agreements & documents → Design & specifications (package type, home style) → Proposal → Selections → Engineering → Contract → Delivery

## Data & Storage
- Dad plans to store files in **AWS**
- Database: working with **Postgres**
- Tammy continues sending documents (forms, screenshots, excels) that inform the system design; waiting for ~90% of information before finalizing

## Video/Meeting Analysis Tool
Dad built a **Jupyter notebook tool** that programmatically extracts insights from recorded meetings:
- Takes a meeting video and its transcript (which has speaker-change timestamps)
- For each timestamp segment, goes to that point in the video and captures screenshots
- Detects screen changes within a segment and captures all distinct screenshots
- Each screenshot is analyzed by Claude to describe its content (saved as JSON)
- Associates screenshots + JSON descriptions + transcript segments together
- Then Claude cross-references what is being said with what is shown on screen to extract process details and data structures

This replaces hours of manual note-taking (3 hours for 1 hour of video manually vs. minutes programmatically).

Plan: **dogfood our own product** using this tool — use it on our upcoming meetings to automatically create documents about each topic discussed, with screenshots and version history.

## Security / Privacy
No HIPAA-level concerns. Standard approach: inform participants that the meeting is being recorded; consent is sufficient.

edits.new_value:
## Sales Process & CRM Vision

- The engagement is focused on building a sales-focused tool for Golden Eagle's salespeople. The most basic version: pre-call prep documents, post-call follow-ups, action items, and data repository/tracking.
- A separate effort (led by a team member's father) is already underway to build a broader CRM / internal system revamp — the sales tool should slot into that larger system over time.
- Want to avoid rebuilding the company from scratch technologically for $15,000. Start narrow on sales, potentially phase into ops and design team integration later.

## Key Discovery Questions (still open)
- How many buyers finance? How much of the sales process involves working with lenders/banks?
- What does the floor plan back-and-forth process look like (architects, design team, interior design, specs, renderings)? Could be a months-long manual process.
- What communication channels do salespeople use with clients? (phone, text, email, LinkedIn?) Do they issue corporate phones?
- Do they have a support email vs. sales email? Is inbound triaged or round-robin?
- Are sales reps on quota?
- How do they take payments?
- Do they have an understanding of what makes a good deal (deal length, budget, lead source, etc.) or is back-end data messy?
- Where do they currently track tasks / project management?

## Product Concepts Discussed (2026-03-30)
- **Sequences / Playbooks**: Enroll prospects into multi-step outreach sequences (email drip campaigns, follow-up cadences). Different playbook types (e.g., "difficult customers" sequence where CEO calls top leads).
- **Prospect lifecycle management**: Each prospect has their own record but also fits into company-wide pipeline stages. Need to see all people in the same stage.
- **AI-assisted workflow**: Salesperson can speak or message naturally (e.g., "just had a great call with Jim") and the system auto-updates records, suggests next steps, generates follow-up actions.
- **Gantt chart integration**: Tammy provided a Gantt chart; this could be embedded in the tool with suggested next steps and action items.
- **Financing as a tracked detail**: Financing status would be a field within project/prospect details.
- **Centralized system**: Avoid multiple disconnected systems sending conflicting outreach. One unified platform.
- **Operational excellence angle**: System helps coach reps, surfaces contacts to reach out to, enforces best practices — "you basically cannot be a low performer because the system will help pull you up."
- **Simple data analysis**: e.g., top 10 historical tactics that closed deals, applied to current pipeline.

## Phasing Ideas
- Phase 1: Sales-specific features (pre-call prep, follow-ups, sequences)
- Phase 2: Integrate ops
- Phase 3: Integrate design team
- Long-term: Entire company trained on unified CRM

## Logistics
- As of 2026-03-30, waiting on interview responses from Golden Eagle team members. Chris Stitcher reached out to coordinate but no one has responded yet.
- Planning to email Chris tonight to push for completing interviews this week.
- A previous Friday call took place with Golden Eagle where Gantt chart functionality was discussed.
